bullet1_requirement.notes: R9 append-only JSONL scoring with CAS fully implemented. Live evidence: pytest 10/10 PASS (tests/generated/20260525-050824/), test-lifecycle-cas.sh 9/9 PASS, test-inject-branches.sh 3/3 PASS, test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py 1/1 PASS. All 19 files in dev.files_modified and dev.files_created confirmed on disk. score-update.sh appends 9-field JSONL under exclusive flock; agent-scores.json write path absent (comment-only references). CAS (--expected-prev-score) exits 3 on mismatch per AC2. lifecycle-baseline-import.sh populates 21 agents idempotently per AC5/AC8. git ls-files exits 0 for logs/lifecycle.jsonl (AC9). SKIP_FILES/SKIP_NAMES updated in both doc_sync files (AC7). The user-stated requirement — eliminate score drift by replacing per-write full-JSON overwrite with append-only JSONL under exclusive flock, with CAS for stale-state detection — is satisfied.

bullet2_ac_coverage.notes: All 10 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC10) independently re-verified by QA via live test execution on this close attempt. AC1 (append 9 mandatory fields): PASS. AC8 (21 canonical agents populated): PASS. AC9 (git ls-files --error-unmatch exits 0): PASS. AC10 (malformed JSONL exits 1 with stderr, no INJECTION_PROOF): PASS. One minor spec_text_vs_execution_drift objection from prior QA cycle (AC8 check field literal text) is non-blocking per original QA report assessment (blocks_cycle: false).

Both previously-blocking violations resolved: (1) score-inject.sh:63-65 flock hardcode fixed by micro-cycle 20260526-042454 (LOCK_TIMEOUT=${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10} confirmed at line 63, flock -s -w ${LOCK_TIMEOUT} at line 65); (2) lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:49,53 flock hardcode fixed by same micro-cycle (LOCK_TIMEOUT=${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10} at line 49, flock -w ${LOCK_TIMEOUT} at line 53). score-update.sh:77 flock -w 10 confirmed pre-existing under provision alpha (present in commit 9d68a38, the task 050824 baseline; not introduced by this diff). Per AC-2.6(b): 0 introduced_in_diff violations across all three inspectors = PASS. Codex confirmed NO_BLOCKERS for both style and cleanliness inspectors.

bullet4_security.notes: No credentials, hardcoded secrets, or absolute paths introduced. flock timeouts use LOCK_TIMEOUT env var with ${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10} default in score-inject.sh (line 63) and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh (line 49). score-update.sh:77 flock -w 10 is pre-existing (provision alpha, introduced before task baseline). agent-scores.json write path absent from score-update.sh (only two comment-only references). No forbidden fields ('note', 'uncapped_delta') emitted in JSONL. All test suites pass live. No regression against existing functionality: test-inject-branches.sh 3/3 PASS confirms score-inject.sh backward compatibility, test_AC4 1/1 PASS confirms test infrastructure integrity. bash -n syntax checks pass for all modified shell scripts.

findings.issue: close-report-20260525-050824.md last line is still 'CLOSE: NO' from the prior second close attempt. Codex warns this would fail downstream /commit close-gate.
findings.proposed_fix_from_codex: Append a new third-attempt close section to the same close-report with CLOSE: YES after QA accepts the current inspector evidence.
findings.qa_resolution: The qa-report-close-20260525-050824.json (THIS file) is the close verdict artifact for the third attempt. The orchestrator's /close workflow reads the qa-report-close JSON for the current attempt's verdict, not the prior close-report file. The prior close-report's CLOSE:NO is expected historical record of the second attempt — it is not consulted for the third attempt's gate. The new close-report (close-report-20260525-050824.md) will be written by the orchestrator based on THIS qa-report-close JSON. Not a blocker for this QA verdict.

findings.issue: Partial-final JSONL crash-recovery: score-update.sh skips a malformed final line but appends without truncating it, which could produce non-final corruption in a subsequent read.
findings.proposed_fix_from_codex: Under exclusive flock, detect malformed final partial lines; truncate from the final-line offset before appending, or exit 2 for malformed final lines. Add a CAS test for partial-final-line recovery.
findings.qa_resolution: Spec Edge Cases section 2 explicitly acknowledges this design tradeoff: 'Partial final JSONL line (mid-write crash): reader skips the unterminated final line silently. Writer uses flock to prevent torn writes in practice; the skip handles crash recovery.' This is a BA-spec-accepted design decision, not a defect. The prior QA already recorded this as out_of_scope_observation (Codex Finding 3 in qa-report-20260525-050824.json). Not a blocker.

findings.issue: Current score-update.sh uses new_score = min(100, unclamped_score) (no lower clamp) while BA ticket M1 specifies max(0, min(100, unclamped_score)). The script header says 'Score has no lower bound (negative scores accumulate), upper bound 100.'
findings.proposed_fix_from_codex: Either restore the lower clamp for this task, or record an AC-deviation/user-need-satisfied rationale and update stale AC/test/completion text.
findings.qa_resolution: QA git-traced the provenance of this change. git show 9d68a38 -- scripts/score-update.sh confirms the pre-task baseline had max(0, min(100, unclamped_score)); task 050824's diff ALSO wrote max(0, min(100, unclamped_score)) per git show 9d68a38's diff line 241 '+new_score = max(0, min(100, unclamped_score))'. The no-lower-bound change (dropping max(0,)) was introduced by the SUBSEQUENT commit 7dd9665 ('chore(commands,scripts) — update close.md, score scripts, lifecycle log and CLAUDE.md'), which postdates task 050824 and was part of a different cycle that introduced the Disgraced rank for scores below 0. Task 050824 is not responsible for this change. Not a blocker for this task's close.

findings.issue: AC3 test weakly wired: fake agent-scores.json is not passed via HOME/default path, so negative discrimination is soft.
findings.proposed_fix_from_codex: Set a temp HOME containing .claude/agent-scores.json with a conflicting BA score.
findings.qa_resolution: OBSERVATION_ONLY per codex itself. The --lifecycle-file isolation mechanism is correctly implemented. test-lifecycle-cas.sh test (e) provides the stronger positive + negative assertion for score-inject reading lifecycle.jsonl. Not a blocker.

findings.issue: score-update.sh:77 flock -w 10 provision alpha correctly applied per git blame; optional future cleanup.
findings.qa_resolution: Confirmed OBSERVATION_ONLY. Provision alpha applies. Pre-existing before task 050824 baseline commit 9d68a38. Not introduced_in_diff.

Codex raised 2 POTENTIAL BLOCKER findings (CF2 partial JSONL crash-recovery, CF3 lower clamp discrepancy) and 1 BLOCKER finding (CF1 prior close-report artifact). QA classified all three as out_of_scope after evidence-based investigation: CF1 is a process artifact resolved by this QA report itself; CF2 is spec-intentional per Edge Cases section 2; CF3 is a post-task change introduced by commit 7dd9665, not by task 050824's diff. Codex verdict after QA resolution: NO_BLOCKERS for this close attempt.
